why: Canonical seed-taxonomy tag (namespace.md:320). Round-trips under the single-segment capability-tag grammar (CAPABILITY_TAG_RE, patterns.ts:21).
Plain lowercase tag, no separators — the simplest valid form.
Two chars is the length floor. Guards the off-by-one at the mandatory trailing [a-z0-9] in CAPABILITY_TAG_RE.
patterns.ts:21 explicitly excludes single-char tags ('none in the seed taxonomy and they collide with 1-char structured-id forms').
The tag alphabet is lowercase-only; uppercase would break case-sensitive NATS-subject and KV-key embedding.
The '--' rejection mirrors DID_RE's negative lookahead (RFC-0001). Keeps tags safe to embed in subjects, KV keys, and file paths.
The mandatory final [a-z0-9] forbids a trailing hyphen — a divergence from stack-slug (RFC-0001), which permits one.
First char MUST be a letter — a segment that could appear in a NATS subject must not start with a digit.
Grill D1: underscores are excluded by ratified kebab-strict (segments project into subjects) — not a C-3 placeholder anymore. federated.subject_dispatch migrates at flag-day R.
FLIPPED by grill D5 (was the §9.4 gap vector): the trust-boundary gate MUST reject a reserved tag before folding. FAILS against the deployed fold-without-validation — the named defect.

HISTORICAL masking case, kept: a shared seed tag passing both pre-convergence grammars hid the C-3 divergence. Post-D1 there is one grammar; the vector now documents why a single shared-tag interop test was never sufficient.

Live cortex production id (audit discovery-econ). VALID under CAPABILITY_ID_REGEX, INVALID as a myelin capability-tag (contains '.'). The C-3 incompatibility made concrete. (Converged grammar D1: compound with capability-tag segments — canonical.)

Grill D1: the converged grammar forbids _ in segments (ratified kebab-strict; segments project into subjects). The live federated.subject_dispatch migrates to federated.subject-dispatch at flag-day R — named defect until then.

cortex sub-capability compound. SEMANTIC HAZARD: a myelin task requiring the tag 'code-review' does NOT match an agent advertising 'code-review.typescript' under the shipped exact-tag match (docs/discovery.md: caps.includes(tag)), though cortex prefix-matching would. Match semantics diverge, not just grammar. (Converged grammar D1: compound with capability-tag segments — canonical.)

Anti-spoof fast-reject (verify.ts:56-60): the signing stamp DID must equal the advertisement actor-DID. An agent cannot advertise another agent's capabilities. (HISTORICAL: F-11 decode-compat only — the wire retires at flag-day R, grill D2.)

R2 transition boundary (advertisement-identity.ts). An advertisement carrying BOTH the deprecated 'principal' and canonical 'identity' actor-DID keys is refused BEFORE canonicalization — a differing pair is an attack, an identical pair a producer bug; either way not silently coalesced. (HISTORICAL: F-11 decode-compat only — the wire retires at flag-day R, grill D2.)

register.ts:58 requires maxConcurrent to be a positive integer. A zero/negative/non-integer ceiling would either exclude the agent from all dispatch or overflow the executor pool. (HISTORICAL: F-11 decode-compat only — the wire retires at flag-day R, grill D2.)

load is clamped to [0,1] at signing time (register.ts clampLoad). NOTE: load is NOT re-validated at verify time (verify.ts) — a hand-forged registration signed off-path could carry an out-of-range load. Documented in §9.1. (HISTORICAL: F-11 decode-compat only — the wire retires at flag-day R, grill D2.)
Converged grammar (D1): dotted compound, each segment a capability-tag. Live id, already conformant.

Segment-prefix rule (D1 sub-decision, §4.2): requirement segments are a prefix of advertised segments. Agrees with the ratified subject matcher (tasks.code-review.> is inclusive). myelin's exact-membership includes() FAILS this — the named defect.
Prefix is directional: a specialized requirement is NOT satisfied by a generalist advertisement.
Equal ids trivially match (prefix of itself).
The rule is SEGMENT-prefix, not string-prefix: code-rev does not match code-review.
Equality-matched v1 (D3, closes §6.5 + RFC-0005 OD-7): identical mode matches.

The 'minimum' ordering the field name implies is RESERVED/undefined (D3): strict does NOT satisfy selective in v1. A future Updates: may widen (compatible); an implementation inventing an ordering today is non-conformant.

Trust-boundary gate (D5, §7): every capabilities[] entry MUST parse as a §4.1 converged id before the announcement folds into the registry. FAILS against the deployed fold-without-validation — the §9.1 named defect.
The canonical wire's normative payload (D2, §7): identity/scope/capabilities/timestamp, each capability a converged id, no session interior (ADR-0005).
